issues.description: FR-1b spec deviation: determine_trigger skips the judge for round < judge_min_round (default 3) when findings include any High or Critical severity. This means the judge cannot override-accept a round-2 verdict where the reviewer over-classified a finding's severity — partially defeating Problem 1 (triviality override). A round-2 verdict with all medium-severity style findings correctly triggers, but adding a single borderline 'high' finding disables the judge entirely. The configurable judge_min_round provides an operator escape hatch but the default behavior silently deviates from FR-1b's stated trigger of verdict.clean == false.
issues.suggestion: Document this as an acknowledged deviation in a code comment referencing the spec, and consider allowing the judge when high-severity count <= 1 and all other findings are low/medium. Alternatively, reduce the default judge_min_round to 2.

issues.description: No integration test covers the cancel path's judge decision backfill. The cancel path was restructured to flow through the unified backfill block in tick(), but there is no test verifying that cancelling a loop with prior judge decisions correctly sets loop_final_state to 'CANCELLED' and loop_terminated_at on those records. This is one of the explicitly listed terminal states in FR-5b.
issues.suggestion: Add an integration test that creates a loop with one or more judge decisions, cancels the loop, and verifies that all judge_decisions rows have loop_final_state='CANCELLED' and loop_terminated_at set.

issues.description: Token budget accounting is tight. MAX_SPEC_CHARS(4K) + MAX_ROUND_CHARS(3K) + MAX_VERDICT_CHARS(2K) = 9K chars for truncatable fields, plus recurring_findings (10 × ~200 = 2K), system prompt (~2K), JSON envelope overhead (~2K) totals ~15K chars (~3.75K tokens). However, the progressive truncation guard uses MAX_INPUT_CHARS=28K which is ~7K tokens — close to the 8K input token budget from FR-4b. The guard threshold should be lower to ensure compliance with the spec's stated limit.
issues.suggestion: Reduce MAX_INPUT_CHARS from 28000 to 24000 (~6K tokens) to provide more headroom within FR-4b's 8K token budget, accounting for the system prompt sent separately.

issues.description: All clean verdicts skip the judge at every round, not just round 1. FR-1c specifically exempts only 'clean == true AND round == 1', implying round 2+ clean verdicts were potentially intended to pass through the judge. The code has a comment documenting this as intentional, which is appropriate, but the spec text is ambiguous.
issues.suggestion: No code change needed — the behavior is reasonable and documented. Consider proposing a spec clarification that all clean verdicts skip the judge.

issues.description: FR-6c asks for judge decisions shown 'inline with each round, so engineers can see why the loop made non-obvious transitions.' The inline display in the round loop is correct, but judge decisions whose round number doesn't match any RoundRecord (edge case from round increment timing) would only appear in the flat API array, which the CLI does not render at all. These orphaned decisions would be invisible to CLI users.
issues.suggestion: Add a final summary line after the rounds loop if any judge_decisions entries were not rendered inline (i.e., had no matching round_summary), e.g., 'N additional judge decisions (use --json for details)'.
